mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the effective federal funds rate over the trailing 3 months lowers the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and reduces the opportunity cost of holding risk assets, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The effect is continuous and does not require a threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or the sign of the correlation flips in more than 40% of rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the triggered-instance mean return is not statistically distinct from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) growth and housing starts (HOUST) growth indicates a broad-based consumer and construction demand expansion that jointly signals durable GDP growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. Neither series alone is sufficient; the signal requires both to be rising together, capturing a compounding demand effect not visible in either series individually.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 2% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with resilient consumer demand
failure_condition: Joint-trigger sample shows 20-day forward return distribution statistically indistinguishable from the unconditional distribution (Kolmogorov-Smirnov p > 0.10), or the interaction effect does not outperform the sum of univariate RSAFS and HOUST signals in an ablation test.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth momentum only translates into positive equity forward returns during late-cycle expansion when capacity utilization pressures support pricing power; in early-cycle recovery the same INDPRO acceleration reflects restocking and does not reliably predict returns. The direction and strength of the INDPRO-return relationship is regime dependent.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion (GDPC1 growth positive but decelerating)
failure_condition: The sign of the INDPRO-forward-return relationship in late-cycle windows matches the sign observed in early-cycle windows in more than 50% of historical cycles, indicating no regime dependence.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) is a continuous coincident proxy for labor income and consumption capacity; positive PAYEMS growth is associated with positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent trading week as markets price in sustained corporate revenue support.
failure_condition: Correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and subsequent 5-day forward SPX returns is not significantly different from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample, or sign flips in over 45% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Once the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, monetary policy is restrictive enough to meaningfully compress valuation multiples and increase the probability of a credit-driven slowdown; above this threshold, further stability or increases in FEDFUNDS are associated with negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as tightening effects propagate to earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Above the 4.5% threshold, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances or the mean return is not significantly below the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with concurrent retail sales (RSAFS) deceleration signals a divergence between backward-looking output strength and forward-looking consumer demand weakening, jointly predicting negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as markets anticipate a demand-driven growth slowdown that GDP alone has not yet captured.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2% AND RSAFS 3-month growth < 0%
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging consumer softness
failure_condition: The joint-trigger subsample's mean 60-day forward return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05), or reversing either single-series condition alone produces equivalent predictive power, indicating no true interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation regime, rising industrial production (INDPRO) is interpreted by markets as a signal of persistent demand pressure that reinforces inflation and monetary tightening risk, producing negative near-term S&P 500 returns; in a low-inflation regime, the same INDPRO increase is read as healthy growth and produces positive returns. The direction of the INDPRO signal flips depending on the CPIAUCSL inflation regime.
regime_hint: high-inflation regime (CPIAUCSL YoY growth above historical median)
failure_condition: The sign of the INDPRO-forward-return relationship in high-inflation-regime windows matches the sign in low-inflation-regime windows in more than 50% of comparable historical periods, indicating the relationship is not regime dependent.
